intent: Prove the reusable redaction boundary strips signed URL query secrets, bearer tokens, JWT-like values, and sensitive object keys.
expected_behavior: Diagnostic shape is preserved, but token-bearing values are replaced with [redacted].

intent: Prove persisted platform observability events do not store signed URLs, open tokens, authorization headers, or nested token values.
expected_behavior: Observed event details and extras are stored with sensitive values redacted.

intent: Prove production app error payloads and development console diagnostics are redacted before persistence or console output.
expected_behavior: Signed URLs, authorization headers, and open tokens do not appear in logged payloads.

intent: Prove the shared logger boundary redacts token-bearing diagnostics before delegating to console methods.
expected_behavior: Console receives sanitized objects and strings while safe metadata remains available.

intent: Prove PDF crash breadcrumbs redact signed URLs, open tokens, error messages, and nested extras before AsyncStorage persistence.
expected_behavior: Stored breadcrumb data contains [redacted] markers and no raw diagnostic secrets.

intent: Guard canonical PDF backend transport behavior while redacting transport diagnostic details.
expected_behavior: Canonical backend invocation behavior is preserved across native and web paths.

intent: Guard PDF document action behavior after diagnostic route/source URI redaction.
expected_behavior: Open path behavior and route semantics remain unchanged.

intent: Guard PDF viewer lifecycle behavior after replacing raw console payloads with redacted helpers.
expected_behavior: Viewer lifecycle remains stable and route-driven behavior is unchanged.

intent: Guard native handoff planning and source-order audit after viewer diagnostic helper extraction.
expected_behavior: Native handoff semantics and source ordering remain unchanged.

intent: Keep module growth explicit after adding one reusable security boundary and focused regression tests.
expected_behavior: Performance budget remains bounded and documented.
